learning_outcomes.description: Apply technical terminology, definitions and calculations used in health-focussed research.
Critically evaluate, and synthesise existing evidence from a range of sources to develop coherent and logical arguments.
Construct informed research questions, aims and objectives to guide a health-based research project.
Assess study designs used to answer clinical questions in oral health research.
Interpret and professionally communicate evidence-based practice knowledge, skills, and ideas to specialist audiences.

Recognise and demonstrate interprofessional collaboration skills including role clarification and negotiation, team functioning and communication (trust, accountability, inclusion, psychological safety), underpinned by relationship-focused-care and services.
Develop and demonstrate the teamwork capabilities required to collaboratively construct a rigorous research proposal.

assessments.task_description:
The assessment will be completed in your timetabled tutorial class, with a final submission date set a week later.

This is a team assessment, and all team members are expected to contribute equally to the team’s assessment items during the tutorial. Teams will be assigned prior to the tutorial. There are two (2) parts to this assessment – a worksheet based upon a client video that your team will choose during the tutorial class, and a team check in with your tutor.

Part One

Part One requires you to complete a worksheet and will draw on the content of the GEMS IPCP module one, focusing on role-clarification and negotiation, team communication, team functioning, and relationship focused care and services

Length: There is no word limit for the worksheet.

 

Part Two

Part Two involves a check-in with a tutor, to assess and reflect on processes of collaboration.

Length: approx. 10 minutes

 

Teams will need to submit Part One in the Assessment tab on the IPCP Learn.UQ course site at the end of the tutorial class. Additional information regarding submission will be provided on the HABS1240 GEMS IPCP Learn.UQ course site.

Please refer to the assessment information on Learn.UQ for further details and requirements for the assessment.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: The IPCP module is a Pass/Fail assessment task. Students who pass the module will receive a course grade based on their passing the remaining must pass assessment/s in the course.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description: These exercises allow students to progressively demonstrate the knowledge and skills that they are acquiring through their studies. Development of knowledge and skills in these sessions has direct relevance to more detailed research-based work within this course and the broader program.  For this task, you are required to complete the assessable exercises that are assigned to the five (5) specified tutorials. Task information and assessment criteria will be made available on the course Blackboard site prior to the assessment.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.category: Paper/ Report/ Annotation, Essay/ Critique, Participation/ Student contribution, Presentation
assessments.task_description:
In this assessment task, you and your partner will delve into the realm of dental research by selecting and critically analysing both a landmark paper and a contemporary paper within a dental discipline. Working collaboratively, you will prepare a seminar presentation to share your findings with an expert audience. Additionally, each student will submit an individual written piece that delves deeper into the analysis of the selected papers.Task information and assessment criteria will be made available on the course Blackboard site prior to the assessment.

The presentation will be recorded and retained as per university policy. The recording will be stored in a secure manner and will only be accessed if required for the purposes of moderation of marking; provision of feedback to the student; and/or re-marking following a successful re-mark application.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Written work and presentation submitted through eportfolio.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description: In this task, you are required to produce a literature review relevant to an oral health research question or topic within a general dental context. Task information and assessment criteria will be made available on the course Blackboard site prior to the assessment.

learning_activities.topic:
Case-based learning

This activity explores seminal landmark papers and contemporary evidence in dentistry to provide a comprehensive understanding of the field’s evolution and current best practices. In this innovative activity, participants will embark on a captivating journey through the evolution of dental literature, dissecting seminal landmark papers and contemporary evidence shaping modern dental practice. Through interactive sessions blending historical perspectives with cutting-edge research, students will delve deep into the foundational principles and latest advancements in various domains of dentistry.

Learning outcomes: L03, L04, L05, L06, L07
learning_activities.topic_description: Case-based learning This activity explores seminal landmark papers and contemporary evidence in dentistry to provide a comprehensive understanding of the field’s evolution and current best practices. In this innovative activity, participants will embark on a captivating journey through the evolution of dental literature, dissecting seminal landmark papers and contemporary evidence shaping modern dental practice. Through interactive sessions blending historical perspectives with cutting-edge research, students will delve deep into the foundational principles and latest advancements in various domains of dentistry.

learning_activities.topic:
Tutorials

The tutorials have been designed to support and strengthen your research writing and basic statistical skills. Participants will learn how to structure and write academic research papers, develop critical analysis and academic writing techniques, and master editing and proofreading. The cohort will also gain an understanding of fundamental statistical concepts, analysis, and interpretation, and learn to apply these skills to answer research questions.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L07
learning_activities.topic_description: Tutorials The tutorials have been designed to support and strengthen your research writing and basic statistical skills. Participants will learn how to structure and write academic research papers, develop critical analysis and academic writing techniques, and master editing and proofreading. The cohort will also gain an understanding of fundamental statistical concepts, analysis, and interpretation, and learn to apply these skills to answer research questions.

learning_activities.topic:
GEMS Interprofessional Collaborative Practice Module

You will work in small teams in the tutorial with students from: Audiology, Dentistry, Dietetics, Nursing, Occupational Therapy, Physiotherapy, Psychology, Social Work and Speech Pathology. There is one module consisting of on-line preparation and a face-to-face tutorial class. You will work in your small team to engage in a range of learning activities to develop your knowledge and skills to help prepare you for working in interprofessional collaborative teams when caring for clients. 

Learning outcomes: L05, L06
